issues.main_description_html:
✂️ Slicing — Restore Page

Slice the Restore Page UI into the new pages-new flow to match the Figma design.

🎨 Design: https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1032-25168&t=lZxYXzRVvZbq7YgV-0

Scope

  • Build the layout/components to match the Figma frame (spacing, typography, color, states).
  • Standalone + ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + Tailwind 4 / Zard UI; semantic tokens (light/dark).
  • Static/dummy data acceptable for slicing — API wiring is a separate task.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open the Restore Page route.

  1. Layout match — compare against Figma: header, inputs, helper text, and primary CTA placement/sizing match the design.
  2. States — empty/filled/focus/error and disabled CTA states render as designed.
  3. Responsive — 375px (mobile) and desktop: no overflow, no horizontal scroll, content reflows cleanly.
  4. Light/Dark — both themes legible; contrast holds; no hardcoded colors that break dark mode.
  5. No errors — no console errors/warnings on load and interaction.

Acceptance: pixel-faithful to Figma; responsive; light + dark; ≥44px touch targets; no console errors.

Demo by developer

  • Standalone + ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + Tailwind 4 / Zard UI; semantic tokens (light/dark).
  • Destructive action uses the designed danger styling; actual delete API wiring is a separate task.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open Settings → Affiliate List → trigger Delete on an affiliate.

  1. Layout match — confirmation dialog (title, message, Delete + Cancel buttons) matches Figma; the affiliate being deleted is identified per the design.
  2. Destructive styling — the Delete/confirm button uses the designed danger (red) styling, visually separated from Cancel.
  3. Dismiss — Cancel, ✕, and backdrop/Esc all close the dialog (UI behavior) without deleting.
  4. Scrim — background is dimmed enough that the dialog is clearly in focus.
  5. Light/Dark + no errors — both themes legible; no console errors.

Acceptance: matches Figma (desktop frame); destructive styling + dismiss affordances correct; light + dark; no console errors.

issues.display_title: Issue #3017: Integrate Delete Affiliate API — DELETE affiliate/:id
issues.main_description_html:
Integrate Delete Affiliate API — DELETE affiliate/:id

Integrate delete affiliate with confirmation.

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-settings-integration.md

Scope

  • Endpoint: DELETE affiliate/:id (base baseURLGoPost) via FatchApiService.delete
  • Delete icon → stopPropagation → open confirm modal, set deletingItem=id
  • Confirm → deleteAffiliate(id) → flush cache → reload → close modal
  • Warning copy: posts using this affiliate will be hidden

🧪 Testing Scenario (for tester)

  1. Hover/tap a card → click delete icon → confirm modal appears (does not open edit).
  2. Click Cancel → modal closes, card remains.
  3. Click Delete → "Deleting..." spinner → card disappears from the grid after success.
  4. Refresh the page → the deleted card no longer appears (cache flushed).
  5. Simulate an API error on delete → failure is indicated; retry works.

Demo by developer

issues.display_title: Issue #3016: Integrate Update Affiliate API — PUT affiliate
issues.main_description_html:
Integrate Update Affiliate API — PUT affiliate

Integrate edit affiliate from the Edit modal.

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-settings-integration.md

Scope

  • Endpoint: PUT affiliate (base baseURLGoPost)
  • ⚠️ Important: FatchApiService.put points to the wrong base URL → use a direct HttpClient.put to ${environment.baseURLGoPost}/affiliate with manual headers (authorization, stage, ec: true)
  • Body: same as create + id
  • Open modal via card click or edit icon → patch form, isEditMode=true, editingValue=id
  • On success: flush cache → reload → close modal

🧪 Testing Scenario (for tester)

  1. Click a card / edit icon → modal opens pre-filled with the item's data.
  2. Change title/description/CTA/category → Save → spinner → card content updates in the grid.
  3. Verify changing the category updates the icon & category_id.
  4. Refresh the page → changes persist (persistence + cache flush).
  5. Simulate an API error on Save → changes are not silently lost; retry works.

#Demo By Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #3015: Integrate Create Affiliate API — POST affiliate
issues.main_description_html:
Integrate Create Affiliate API — POST affiliate

Integrate create affiliate from the Add modal.

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-settings-integration.md

Scope

  • Endpoint: POST affiliate (base baseURLGoPost) via FatchApiService.post
  • Body: { title, category_id, url, description, cta_text, ec: true }
  • Apply mapLabelToCategoryId(label) before submit
  • On success: flushRedis([{ path: 'affiliate/list', cacheId: '' }]) → reload list (current search term) → close modal
  • Smart title parse from URL (parseProductTitle) on add

🧪 Testing Scenario (for tester)

  1. Click [+ Add] → modal opens with empty/default form.
  2. Paste a valid https://... URL → valid checkmark appears & title auto-fills.
  3. Select category, fill title/description/CTA → click Save → "Saving..." spinner → new card appears in the grid after success.
  4. Verify the selected category persists with the correct category_id.
  5. Non-http(s) URL → submit rejected (URL validation).
  6. Submit with required fields empty → submit stays disabled / markAllAsTouched.
  7. After create, refresh the page → data persists (confirms persistence + cache flush).

Demo By Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #2922: [Integration] Auth OTP & Reset Password — Logic, Bug Fixes & Specs
issues.main_description_html:
Integration — OTP & Reset Password Logic & Bug Fixes

TypeScript refactor, logic/bug fixes, API wiring, and spec fixes for the auth OTP and Reset Password sub-flow. UI markup is handled in the companion Slicing task.

Source brainstorm: docs/05-06-26/brainstorming/2026-06-05-auth-otp-reset-password.md
Paths: src/app/core/auth/otp/ · src/app/core/auth/reset-password/

Spec fixes (unblock test runs)

  • Bug #1 / #2 — otp.spec.ts & reset-password.spec.ts: move standalone components from declarations: [] to imports: []; add HttpClientTestingModule and TranslateModule.forRoot() (hard crash).

Lifecycle & countdown

  • Bug #3 — reset-password.component.ts: add ngOnDestroy to unsubscribe interval(1000) (memory leak).
  • Bug #4 / #5 — resendOtp() in both components: unsubscribe the old interval before starting a new one (duplicate countdown ticks).
  • Bug #15 — otp.component.ts: initial interval in ngAfterViewInit starts at countDownSecond = 0 and terminates immediately; OTP countdown should start on first resend click (900s).
  • Reset Password countdown starts immediately on ngAfterViewInit (900s) — user arrives with an active OTP.

Logic / wiring

  • Bug #6 — reset-password.component.html resend button is hardcoded [zDisabled]="true"; wire it to resendOtp() + countdown state so it's functional.
  • Bug #7 — otp.component.ts: openCaptcha() sets showCaptcha(true) before checking loadingCountdown(); reorder so the captcha widget initializes correctly.
  • Flow A (showPasswordVerificationUser() = true): Resend → authService.resendOtp(); Verify → checkOTP() → loginAuth() → getUserDetail().
  • Flow B (showPasswordVerificationUser() = false): Resend → openCaptcha() → grecaptcha → resendOtp() → forgotPassword() (captcha gate Flow B only); Verify → checkOTP() → showResetPassword = true.

Dead code & code smells

  • Bug #10 — remove unused declare let moment: any in both .ts.
  • Bug #11 — reset-password.component.ts: remove dead email and inputDisabled signals.
  • Bug #12 — replace deprecated .subscribe(successFn, errorFn) with observer-object pattern in both .ts.
  • Bug #13 — reset-password.component.ts: rename ConfirmedValidator to camelCase per Angular convention.
  • Bug #14 — reset-password.component.ts: stop mutating the API response (res['statusCode'] = 200).

Dependencies

Spec fixes unblock test runs; logic wiring supports the Slicing task's resend countdown and flow-conditional rendering.

✂️ Slicing — Responsive Reset Password

Slice the responsive (mobile) Reset Password flow into the new pages-new flow to match the Figma designs (initial → after Send OTP → after Verify).

🎨 Design — base: https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1406-40022&t=X0ExDK6nFUt8rQdT-0
🎨 Design — after Send OTP: https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1406-40166&t=X0ExDK6nFUt8rQdT-0
🎨 Design — after Verify: https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1407-40456&t=X0ExDK6nFUt8rQdT-0

Scope

  • Build the three mobile states of the Reset Password flow per the Figma frames: (1) request (email/identifier + Send OTP), (2) OTP entry (after Send OTP), (3) new-password (after Verify).
  • Standalone + ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + Tailwind 4 / Zard UI; semantic tokens (light/dark).
  • Visible labels, field states, and transitions per the design — API (send OTP / verify / reset) wiring is a separate task.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open the Reset Password flow at mobile width (375px).

  1. State 1 — request — layout matches the base frame (identifier field, Send OTP CTA); field states render as designed.
  2. State 2 — after Send OTP — OTP entry view matches the design (OTP inputs, resend, verify CTA).
  3. State 3 — after Verify — new-password view matches the design (password + confirm, show/hide, submit).
  4. No overflow + keyboard — no horizontal scroll at 375px; with keyboard open the primary action stays reachable.
  5. Light/Dark + no errors — both themes legible; no console errors.

Acceptance: all three mobile states match Figma; field/transition states correct; no horizontal scroll; keyboard reachable; light + dark; no console errors.

🔌 Integrate — Upload File From Dropbox (Live)

Wire Dropbox import as a panorama source in the new pages-new Create flow (Step 1 · Image).

Component: pages-new/create/upload/*. Live Dropbox chooser (not mock).

Scope

  • Open the Dropbox chooser, let the user pick an image, fetch it, and feed it into the same validation + upload pipeline as local files.
  • Loading/success/error states; no any.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open /create, Step 1.

  1. Open Dropbox — tap the Dropbox option. Expect: the live Dropbox chooser opens and authenticates.
  2. Select file — choose a panorama. Expect: file is fetched and enters the upload pipeline (preview + upload request).
  3. Validation — pick a non-panorama/oversize file. Expect: same validation/error as local files.
  4. Cancel — close the chooser without selecting. Expect: no change, no error.
  5. Error — network/Dropbox error. Expect: clear error feedback; can retry.

Acceptance: live Dropbox chooser works; imported file runs through validation + upload; cancel/error handled; no any.

issues.display_title: Issue #2931: [Fullscreen][Integration] Wire pages-new/fullscreen — models, state sync, navigation & actions
issues.main_description_html:
Module

[Module] 18. Fullscreen · Type: Integration

Context

Wire data, state and behavior for the new fullscreen page at src/app/pages-new/fullscreen/, against the UI built in the companion Slicing task. Hybrid Incremental Parity approach — migrate feature behavior slice by slice and verify each.

Source: brainstorming doc docs/05-06-26/brainstorming/2026-06-05-fullscreen.md

Scope (Integration)

  • Models & facade — typed models (no any) + facade/service loading panorama + multiple data
    • Local state: dataPanorama, multiplePanorama, listDataMultiple, hideMultiple, showRange
  • Slice C (state) — Multiple panorama state sync: tray selection → active dataPanorama, keep tray + stage in sync
  • Slice D — Prev/next navigation + baseline preload of adjacent panorama(s); guard first/last/empty
  • Slice E (wiring) — Action rail social/menu actions + profile card actions wiring
  • Slice F — Typing hardening (remove any remaining any) + regression checks vs pages-old/fullscreen

Out of Scope (next phase)

Full iframe/video parity, cross-feature global service refactors, full preload optimization, decommission of old fullscreen.

Definition of Done

  • Panorama + multiple data load; tray selection and prev/next interactions are stable.
  • Action rail / menu actions behave per parity (no legacy alert placeholders).
  • No any within new fullscreen scope; lint/build/test pass for the change scope.

issues.display_title: Issue #3014: Integrate List Affiliate API — GET affiliate/list
issues.main_description_html:
Integrate List Affiliate API — GET affiliate/list

Integrate the list affiliate endpoint on the settings/affiliate-list page.

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-settings-integration.md

Scope

  • Endpoint: GET affiliate/list (base baseURLGo) via FatchApiService
  • Params: limit=100, page=1, search=''
  • Headers: authorization, ec: true
  • Encrypted response → runDecryption() → data.data_affiliate[]
  • Map to UI model via mapApiToAffiliateItem()
  • Server-side search, 300ms debounce + distinctUntilChanged
  • State signals: affiliates, loading, error (skeleton, empty, error+retry)

🧪 Testing Scenario (for tester)

  1. Open /settings/affiliate-list → list loads and decrypts, cards render with correct data.
  2. On initial load → 3 skeleton cards appear.
  3. Type in the search box → request waits ~300ms (debounce), results filter server-side.
  4. Search with no match → Empty state shows ("No affiliates found").
  5. Disconnect network / API error → Error state + Retry button; click Retry → list reloads.
  6. Verify category_id 1/2/3 maps to the correct label & icon (Tickets/Hotels/Flights).

Demo by developer

screen-capture.27.webm

issues.display_title: Issue #2915: [place-list] Slicing: render item-photographi, location-unavailable header
issues.main_description_html:
Scope: Slicing (UI / markup / visual)

UI-layer work for the Place List (home left panel). Data/API wiring lives in the companion Integration issue.

  • Page: src/app/pages-new/home/place/
  • Components: PlaceComponent, item-place, item-photographi
  • Source: brainstorming doc docs/05-06-26/brainstorming/2026-06-05-place-list.md

Tasks

  • Render item-photographi. The component exists in the folder but is not imported or rendered in place.component.html, so the photographer section never appears. Import and render it below the places list, using data from HOME_PHOTOGRAPHERS. (Hire-button behavior is wired in the Integration issue.)
  • "Location unavailable" header state (polish) — when geolocation fails, show "Location unavailable" in the header instead of silently implying the fallback city.

Patterns to keep

  • ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush — do not remove.

Demo by developer

When the browser does not enable location services, the default location displayed is the one with the highest frequency.

issues.display_title: Issue #2962: Slicing Responsive Follower and Following List
issues.main_description_html:
✂️ Slicing — Responsive Follower & Following List

Slice the responsive (mobile) Follower & Following lists UI (Profile) into the new pages-new flow to match the Figma designs.

🎨 Design — Follower (mobile): https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1405-36787&t=gSkJX9C5Bz4c1AKk-0
🎨 Design — Following (mobile): https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1405-37130&t=gSkJX9C5Bz4c1AKk-0

Scope

  • Adapt the Follower & Following lists to the mobile breakpoint per the Figma frames (full-width rows, mobile spacing, mobile tab/header).
  • Standalone + ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + Tailwind 4 / Zard UI; semantic tokens (light/dark).
  • Static/dummy data acceptable for slicing — API wiring is a separate task.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open Profile → Followers / Following at mobile width (375px).

  1. Mobile layout match — both lists reflow to the mobile design; rows full-width; spacing/tabs match Figma.
  2. No overflow — no horizontal scroll at 375px; long names truncate as designed.
  3. Tab switch — Follower ↔ Following switching works and matches the mobile design.
  4. Touch targets — action buttons are ≥44px and not cramped.
  5. Light/Dark + no errors — both themes legible; no console errors.

Acceptance: both mobile frames match Figma; no horizontal scroll; tab switch + ≥44px targets correct; light + dark; no console errors.

issues.display_title: Issue #2961: Slicing Follower and Following List
issues.main_description_html:
✂️ Slicing — Follower & Following List

Slice the Follower list and Following list UI (Profile) into the new pages-new flow to match the Figma designs.

🎨 Design — Follower: https://www.figma.com/design/RSNRjxqnnr1O24SjpeoPI3/New-PANORRA-360?node-id=1288-37394&t=GcoCoCKkS4LB7men-0

Scope

  • Build the Follower and Following lists (tabs or views: avatar, name/@handle, follow/unfollow action) to match the Figma frames.
  • Standalone + ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + Tailwind 4 / Zard UI; semantic tokens (light/dark).
  • Static/dummy data acceptable for slicing — API wiring is a separate task.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open a Profile → Followers / Following.

  1. Layout match — both lists (Follower + Following) match Figma: row = avatar, name/@handle, action button.
  2. Tab/view switch — switching between Follower and Following shows the correct list/styling per the design.
  3. Action state — follow/unfollow (and "following") button states render as designed; tapping toggles the visual state.
  4. Long names + empty — long names truncate as designed; empty list shows the designed empty state.
  5. Light/Dark + no errors — both themes legible; no console errors.

Acceptance: both lists match Figma; tab switch + action states correct; empty/truncation handled; light + dark; no console errors.

issues.display_title: Issue #2916: [place-list] Integration: reverse geocoding, search filter, hire handler
issues.main_description_html:
Scope: Integration (data / API / logic / behavior)

Data-flow, API wiring, and behavior work for the Place List (home left panel). UI-only rendering lives in the companion Slicing issue.

  • Page: src/app/pages-new/home/place/
  • Component: PlaceComponent (geolocation + orchestration)
  • Source: brainstorming doc docs/05-06-26/brainstorming/2026-06-05-place-list.md

Tasks

  • Bug + Reverse geocoding — dynamic location name. The template statically displays "Toronto" and "Yonge-Bay Corridor" regardless of the user's real location. After coordinates are obtained, call a geocoding API to resolve city/district and bind it dynamically to the location header.
  • Wire onHirePhotographer(). The handler exists but does nothing. At minimum open an info sheet or navigate to the photographer's profile.
  • Make search functional. <app-search> renders UI only with no binding. Connect the search input to a computed() filter over facade.places().
  • Debounce geolocation call (polish) — prevent double-fetches if the component re-initializes.

Patterns to keep

  • Always use HomeFacade for API calls — PlaceComponent must not inject FatchApiService directly.
  • isPlatformBrowser(this.platformId) before accessing geolocation — SSR-safe, must be preserved.
  • ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush — do not remove.

Demo by Developer

map-search.webm

🔌 Integrate — Notification User

Wire the User notifications (follow/mention) to the backend in the new pages-new notification flow.

Pairs with the layout task Create Notification User New Layout (#3032).

Scope

  • Fetch user-type notifications via a facade/service; decrypt + map; render with pagination; mark-as-read; follow-back / view-profile actions. No BehaviorSubject, no any.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

Pre: open Notifications.

  1. Load — user notifications fetch and render (actor, action, action button, time).
  2. Pagination — scroll/load more appends without duplicates.
  3. Action — follow-back / view-profile fires the right request / navigation; button state updates.
  4. Read — marking read updates state + count.
  5. Empty/error — empty state when none; network fail → clear message.

Acceptance: notifications fetched via facade; action + read + pagination; empty/error handled; no any.

issues.display_title: Issue #3020: Detail page affiliate read path
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Post detail must display the same affiliate the creator selected (currently static/partial). Depends on the post detail API exposing affiliate/hotspot data.

Tasks:

  1. Render affiliate banner from the post payload via detail-extra-content.model.ts (not a static mock).
  2. Share the visual contract with create's affiliate-banner-preview (title, description, CTA, external link).
  3. Consider a shared affiliate-banner-panel component (referenced in pages-new-integration-brainstorming.md).
  4. CTA opens item.linkUrl in a new tab.

Open question: should the banner CTA track clicks (analytics endpoint) before redirect?

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-integration.md — Gaps & Open Work, Priority 3.

🧪 Scenario Testing (for tester)

  • Open a published post that has an affiliate banner → confirm the banner renders with correct title, description, and CTA.
  • Click the CTA → confirm it opens linkUrl in a new tab.
  • Open a post with no affiliate → confirm no banner / empty block is shown.
  • Verify visual parity between the detail banner and create's affiliate-banner-preview.
  • Open a post on both mobile and desktop widths → confirm the banner layout is correct.

Demo by developer

screen-capture.40.webm

Demo By Developer

screen-capture.39.webm

Demo by developer

screen-capture.38.webm

Migrate the collection detail page (/collection/:slug) from the legacy module (pages-old/collection-detail) to the pages-new architecture, preserving the existing experience while adopting modern Angular patterns (standalone, signals, OnPush, inject(), strict typing).

Entry point: clicking a collection card in profile (app-list-collection) → /collection/:slug (route repointed to the pages-new component, replacing the old module).

Features to migrate (parity)

  • Render media by post type: panorama (app-view-panorama), video (app-youtube-player), embed iframes (villume, gvt, tourmkr, youtube)
  • Prev/next navigation between collection items (with neighbor-page caching)
  • User strip (avatar, fullname, location) + back button
  • Social actions: like, comment, view count
  • Action menu (Share + Delete) with ownership awareness (owner vs non-owner)
  • Multiple panorama strip + zoom slider
  • Filter & aspect ratio controls
  • Like list modal + share popup (Zard dialog)
  • Edit collection & edit cover (owner)
  • Loading / error / empty & edge states

Technical notes

  • Keep URL /collection/:slug (backward compatible)
  • history.state.collectionData is only a fast-path seed; always validate/fetch from API by slug
  • Preserve runDecryption + required auth headers (authorization, stage, role, v, ec)
  • Avoid direct DOM manipulation; replace alert() with Zard dialogs/toasts; remove any

Reference: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-17-collection-detail.md

🧪 Test Scenarios (for Tester)

  1. Entry flow: from profile, click a collection card → /collection/:slug opens the new page (pages-new), not the old one.
  2. Direct access: refresh / open the URL directly → page still loads (fetched from API, not dependent on history.state).
  3. Media: panorama item renders in viewer; video/embed loads the correct player/iframe; unsupported type → safe fallback.
  4. Prev/Next: prev/next changes item and updates metadata; Next disabled on last item, Prev disabled on first.
  5. Social actions: like updates the UI count and persists after refresh.
  6. Action menu: owner sees Share + Delete; non-owner sees Share only. Delete success → returns to profile & list refreshes.
  7. Error/edge: invalid/deleted slug → unavailable/not-found page; API error → error state with retry + back.
  8. Mobile: controls & overlays remain usable on small screens.

Acceptance: share popup + correct URL; copy + channels work; dismiss behaves; no any.

issues.display_title: Issue #2825: Create function service hostpot three js
Issue #3066: Integrate API Add Post Complete V1
issues.main_description_html:
🔌 Integrate API — Add Post Complete V1

Wire the new pages-new create flow to the v1 complete endpoint, adding Hotspot + Banner and location grouping support.

Based on pages-new create flow — create.facade.ts (completePost) / create.component.ts (completePostFlow).

Description

Add Hotspot and Banner, add location grouping functionality to BE.

  • The mention hotspot feature can use the Search Mention User API to display a list of users.
  • The banner feature can use the List Affiliate API to display a list of affiliates.
  • Postman: https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/26723813/2sBXwsLA3d#ec810a2c-4f8d-4139-bae9-99e0d8b2a81e
  • Endpoint: {{base_url}}/v1/posts/complete
  • BE issue: GoThruMedia/panorra-api-new#345

Scope

  • Extend the complete payload + mapper (create.mapper.ts) to include hotspots (mention/type/title/description/CTA/link), the affiliate banner, and per-location grouping per the v1 contract.
  • Wire mention list via Search Mention User API; affiliate list via List Affiliate API.
  • Facade call to v1/posts/complete; no BehaviorSubject, no any.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

  1. Publish with hotspots — add hotspots (incl. a mention from the user search), publish. Expect: v1/posts/complete payload includes the hotspots; post saves.
  2. Publish with banner — select an affiliate banner, publish. Expect: banner included in payload.
  3. Location grouping — multi-panorama/location post groups locations per the v1 contract in the payload.
  4. Mention search — the mention picker lists users from the Search Mention User API.
  5. Error — BE rejects payload → clear error; no partial silent success; retry possible.

Acceptance: v1 complete payload carries hotspots + banner + location grouping; mention/affiliate lists wired; error handled; no any.

issues.display_title: Issue #3018: Wire create flow to real affiliate list
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Create post Step 2 still uses 3 hardcoded affiliateOptions in CreateComponent. Users who manage affiliates in /settings/affiliate-list don't see them when posting. Wire the create flow to the real list.

Tasks:

  1. Inject AffiliateService into CreateComponent (or a thin CreateAffiliateFacade).
  2. Load affiliates on init or when entering Step 2.
  3. Map AffiliateItem[] → CreateAffiliateOption[] (reuse mapAffiliateItemToCreateOption).
  4. Empty state in affiliate-select: link to /settings/affiliate-list ("Add your first affiliate").
  5. Remove the hardcoded mock after wire-up to avoid drift between settings and create.

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-integration.md — Gaps & Open Work, Priority 1.

🧪 Scenario Testing (for tester)

  • Add an affiliate in Settings → open Create → Step 2 → confirm the new affiliate appears in the affiliate-select dropdown without redeploy.
  • Edit an affiliate in Settings → reopen Create → confirm the dropdown shows updated title/description/CTA.
  • Delete an affiliate → confirm it no longer appears in the Create dropdown.
  • With zero affiliates → confirm empty state shows the "Add your first affiliate" link and it navigates to /settings/affiliate-list.
  • Verify the old 3 mock options (1st Affiliate, etc.) are gone.
  • Verify affiliate-banner-preview reflects the selected real affiliate's fields.

Description

Affiliate selection in create is UI-only — hotspots/banners are not sent in the legacy addPostComplete base64 payload. Align with backend on a contract and integrate the publish path.

Backend contract needed for:

  • affiliate_id (or full snapshot: { title, description, cta, url })
  • panorama position (yaw, pitch)
  • banner vs hotspot kind

Tasks:

  1. Confirm backend contract: affiliate banners in addPostComplete, a separate hotspot endpoint, or post-edit after publish.
  2. Extend create.mapper.ts to include hotspot/banner data once the contract is known.
  3. Until then: keep console.log of the hotspot payload on publish (per original brainstorming rule) or document as blocked.

Open question: snapshot vs reference — send only affiliate_id or full snapshot in case the user edits the affiliate later?

Source: docs/brainstorming/2026-06-15-affiliate-integration.md — Gaps & Open Work, Priority 2. Currently blocked on backend contract.

🧪 Scenario Testing (for tester)

  • Add a banner hotspot with an affiliate in Create → publish → confirm payload includes affiliate_id, yaw/pitch, and kind (verify via network tab once API exists; until then verify console.log).
  • Add multiple hotspots (mention / comment / affiliate mix) → confirm only the correct affiliate banner data is sent.
  • Publish without any hotspot → confirm no affiliate payload is sent and the post still succeeds.
  • Move the banner position on the panorama → confirm updated yaw/pitch are reflected in the payload.

🔌 Integrate — Rename Bookmark Folder

Wire the rename bookmark folder action to the backend in the new pages-new flow.

Pairs with layout task Update Popup Rename Bookmark Folder New Layout (#3057).

Scope

  • Submit the new folder name via a facade/service; update the folder in the list on success; loading/error states. No BehaviorSubject, no any.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

  1. Rename — change the name, save. Expect: request fires; folder name updates in the list.
  2. Validation — empty/unchanged name blocks save.
  3. Cancel — aborts with no API call.
  4. Error — network fail → error toast; entered name preserved.
  5. No double-submit — button disabled while saving.

Acceptance: rename via facade; in-list update + validation; cancel/error handled; no any.

Wire the remove/delete bookmarked post action to the backend in the new pages-new flow.

🧪 Test scenario (for QA)

  1. Delete — confirm removal. Expect: request fires; on success the post leaves the folder.
  2. Cancel — aborts with no API call.
  3. Counts — folder count decrements.
  4. Error — network fail → error toast; post stays.
